items.title: 'I've got my visa back': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia

children.content: Edward James Kilbey's permanent visa was cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for molesting a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has reinstated it due to the 83-year-old UK man's "ill health" and "strong ties" to Australia.

gallery.altText: A man wearing sunglasses on a swing at a children's playground.
A man wearing glasses stands next to a person with a blurred face.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing sunglasses on a swing at a children's playground.
A man wearing glasses stands next to a person with a blurred face.

items.title: House empty more than a year after 69-year-old renter forced out

children.content: After a lifetime of caring for the elderly, Linda Braam is left battling for a place to call home.

image.altText: An older woman in glasses looks sternly at the camera, as she holds the handle of the front door to a house.

items.title: Unusable road may be the end of remote community as food supplies dwindle

children.content: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

image.altText: A damaged road with red sand on either side.

items.title: Food charities struggle to help as fuel cost crisis bites

children.content: From shrinking their hampers to closing waitlists, food charities in Western Australia are adapting their services to meet a surge in demand as more people grapple with rising costs.

gallery.altText: Boxes full of fruit and vegetables.
Bev Woolhouse poses with a serious expression next to a basket of fruit.
A woman shopping for fresh produce in a supermarket.

thumbnail.altText: Boxes full of fruit and vegetables.
Bev Woolhouse poses with a serious expression next to a basket of fruit.
A woman shopping for fresh produce in a supermarket.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: Perth man pays it forward by paying for other motorists' fuel

children.content: WA construction company owner Monty Van der Berg wants to ease the cost of living crisis for Australians, by paying for their fuel one tank at a time.

image.altText: a man and woman smile into the camera

items.title: AC Milan, Inter and Juventus to play matches in Perth

children.content: Perth will be the centre of Italian preseason football in August, with some of the game's heavyweights heading to the Western Australian capital ahead of the next European season.

image.altText: Opposing players compete for the soccer ball, while another player loiters in support

items.title: Human bones found on Rottnest Island, investigation underway

children.content: Police are investigating the origin of what are believed to be several human bones which were found on Rottnest Island.

gallery.altText: A police vehicle parked on a building site.
Rottnest Channel Swim banner, empty.

thumbnail.altText: A police vehicle parked on a building site.
Rottnest Channel Swim banner, empty.

items.title: FOGO waste collection dubbed 'expensive failure'

children.content: Western Australia's second largest city is investigating the viability of the FOGO waste system amid rising costs, with more than a year's worth of waste trucked to landfill.

image.altText: Residential council bins with City of Bunbury branding

items.title: Drunk driver jailed over hit-and-run death in Mandurah

children.content: An intoxicated driver who killed a woman in a hit-and-run in Mandurah, and was allowed to drive two months later, is sentenced to more than four years in prison.

image.altText: A smiling young woman with short dark hair and glasses, dressed in black, sits on a bench chair against a wall with artwork.

items.title: 'There in 10 seconds': Dog rescued after being dragged underwater by crocodile

children.content: Pet kelpie Milli had a lucky escape and survived with only minor injuries after being attacked by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

gallery.altText: man holding a dog
A black kelpie sitting on a concrete floor with a bandaged hind leg.
dog with a bandaged leg.

thumbnail.altText: man holding a dog
A black kelpie sitting on a concrete floor with a bandaged hind leg.
dog with a bandaged leg.

items.title: Top neuroscientist slams AFL move to back new headgear

children.content: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

gallery.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

thumbnail.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?

children.content: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

image.altText: Fuel bowser with a big truck in the background.

items.title: ASX set to slip as doubts about US-Iran ceasefire grow

children.content: Australian shares are set to open lower after climbing yesterday following the US and Iran agreeing to a two-week ceasefire that's now on the brink of collapse.

image.altText: trump nato wtf

items.title: Vehicle rollover on WA mine site kills worker, seriously injures another

children.content: A 38-year-old man dies in a crash on an access road to the King of the Hills mine site, near Leonora.

gallery.altText: The view from a lookout at a gold mine showing the entrance to an underground portal.

thumbnail.altText: The view from a lookout at a gold mine showing the entrance to an underground portal.

items.title: Failed referendum inspires two top dance companies to join forces

children.content: Sixteen dancers from Bangarra Dance Theatre and 19 ballet dancers from the Australian Ballet have joined forces for Flora, choreographed by Bangarra artistic director Frances Rings.

image.altText: On stage, an Indigenous dancer leans back, stretching one arm up. Behind her other dancers in shadow form a line.

items.title: Early season brilliance puts Gawn on track for record All-Australian nod

children.content: Melbourne captain Max Gawn shows no sign of slowing down as an excellent first month of the season puts him front and centre of ABC Sport's rolling AFL All-Australian team.

image.altText: Max Gawn pokes his tongue out at the camera

items.title: Test your knowledge with our classic Australian TV quiz

children.content: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

image.altText: Ray Meagher on the set of Home and Away playing Alf Stewart.

items.title: Claims wild-caught Australian seafood will soon 'only be afforded by the rich'

children.content: Australian seafood prices are likely to rise due to fuel supply issues, with some warning that wild-caught seafood could become unaffordable for most.

image.altText: Prawns at a seafood market

items.title: Court sets aside convictions of parents for starving daughter, grants bail

children.content: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

gallery.altText: A digitally produced image featuring a man and a woman covering their heads with jackets, and a slightly built girl.
A man and woman walking down the street, covering their heads with jackets
Three women with blurred faces walk down stairs outside a court. One is young and two are older.

items.title: Mining on the Moon is closer than ever, but challenges remain

children.content: A scientist, an engineer, an archaeologist and a lawyer break down some of the physical, environmental, ethical and legal challenges of mining on the Moon.

image.altText: The surface of the Moon, with Earth rising in the background.

items.title: Faldo says he will not comment on rival Norman until after Masters

children.content: Respectfully biting his tongue, Nick Faldo is refusing to dignify fresh criticism from Greg Norman that is certain to reignite one of golf's fiercest rivalries.

image.altText: Nick Faldo and Greg Norman stand together on the tee, in front of a huge gallery of fans

items.title: What's in 'Australian Made' products? Not necessarily Australian produce

children.content: Farmers battling cheap imported produce want a review of food labelling laws to highlight what Australian produce is included in products.

image.altText: A farmer amongst his soybean crop.

items.title: A 'bunch of nobodies' changed Australia — now a new generation carries their torch

children.content: When a "bunch of nobodies" in Sydney decided to stand up to police brutality, no-one could guess their impact would still be felt more than 50 years later.

image.altText: A black and white photo of people standing in front of a shopfront.

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

gallery.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.
A man wearing a dark polo shirt sits on a park bench with his back turned to the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.
A man wearing a dark polo shirt sits on a park bench with his back turned to the camera.

items.title: Distance triples and comeback kings end Aus Open swimming

children.content: Sam Williamson capped his comeback with victory in the 50m breaststroke just 11 months after he suffered a career-threatening injury.

image.altText: Sam Williamson swims breaststroke

items.title: As Hull chases elusive gold, Hollingsworth is looming over her shoulder

children.content: Olympic silver medallist Jess Hull is used to setting the benchmark in Australian middle-distance running, but Claudia Hollingsworth is coming for her 1,500-metre crown at this weekend's National Championships.

image.altText: Jess Hull and Claudia Hollingsworth stand back to back with arms folded, smiling, Sydney Opera House in background

items.title: Team behind cult 2012 video game reunites on Grammy winner

children.content: Art director Matt Nava and composer Austin Wintory reveal how their cult 2012 video game Journey shaped their latest offering, the Grammy Award-winning Sword of the Sea.

image.altText: A video game character grinds their board along a large metal chain in what appears to be an underwater ruined city

items.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death

children.content: Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time about the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, saying he and his children have since been "harassed" and "stalked" by the media.

image.altText: Rohan Dennis wearing formal attire walks along a sandstone wall, with a broadcast camera partially visible.

items.title: Driver 'didn't have time to brake' before crash that killed boy on scooter

children.content: An 11-year-old boy dies after his scooter collides with a car in Mandurah, with a witness saying they saw the boy riding down a small hill onto the road before the crash — WA's fourth road fatality over the Easter period.
